rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical anodic oxidation of an α-methylstyrene-type substrate leading to benzylic/allylic hydroxylation (formation of 2) versus deprotonation to a diene-type product (3), with TEMPO trapping evidence for a radical-polar crossover via a benzylic cation intermediate INT2.
electrochemical_role: Anodic single-electron oxidation of the styrene substrate generates a styrene radical cation (INT1); after TEMPO radical rebound a closed-shell benzylic cation (INT2) is formed, which is then trapped by H2O (hydroxylation) or deprotonated by lutidine (diene formation). A second anodic oxidation of 2 leads onward to ketone product 4.

key_intermediates.name: oxidized bis-aminoxy/oxocarbenium INT3 en route to 4

electron_flow: Anode removes one electron from the styrene to give a radical cation INT1; TEMPO couples to the terminal carbon (TS_radical, 1.7 kcal/mol) producing the benzylic cation INT2 (-23.8 kcal/mol), which partitions between H2O addition (benzylic vs terminal hydroxylation, giving 2 or 2') and lutidine-mediated deprotonation to diene 3; subsequent anodic oxidation of 2 generates INT3 that collapses to ketone 4 with release of TMP+.
rate_determining_step: On the productive surface, the lutidine/H2O-assisted ionic addition/deprotonation step from INT2 (TS-OH-benzyl 11.3, TS-diene 13.7, TS-OH-term 14.3 kcal/mol) is rate-limiting; the prior TEMPO radical-rebound is nearly barrierless (1.7 kcal/mol).

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: DFT shows near-barrierless radical rebound (TS_radical = 1.7 kcal/mol) onto the styrene radical cation to give the strongly stabilized adduct INT2 (-23.8 kcal/mol), supporting a stepwise radical-then-cation (radical-polar crossover) pathway over a direct ionic addition (TS_ionic = 19.6 kcal/mol).

probe_reagents.name: 2,6-lutidine
probe_reagents.observation: Acts as Brønsted base in the deprotonation pathway (TS-diene, 13.7 kcal/mol) to deliver diene product 3, and as proton shuttle with H2O in the hydroxylation TSs.

probe_reagents.name: H2O
probe_reagents.observation: Nucleophile that traps cationic INT2 at the benzylic position (TS-OH-benzyl 11.3 kcal/mol, leading to 2 at -26.4) preferentially over the terminal position (TS-OH-term 14.3, leading to 2' at -19.5).

notes: Two competing mechanistic surfaces were computed: (i) a 'radical trapping / radical-polar crossover' route via TEMPO addition to the styrene radical cation forming closed-shell cation INT2, and (ii) a direct 'charge annihilation' ionic route via TS_ionic (19.6 kcal/mol) leading to 2'. The radical-polar crossover (favored) is far lower in energy. From INT2, three downstream pathways were compared: benzylic hydroxylation (favored, TS = 11.3, giving 2), deprotonation to diene 3 (TS = 13.7), and terminal hydroxylation (TS = 14.3, giving 2'). Subsequent oxidation of 2 generates ketone 4 with extrusion of TMP+ (Figure S19). NPA charge analysis on INT2 (C1 +0.21, C2 +0.37, C3 +0.21) rationalizes site selectivity.
